title: tragacantha mossulensis
intro: Tragacantha mossulensis, also known as Senna italica or Alexandrian senna, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions, traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its laxative and purgative effects. The plant's leaves and pods contain anthraquinone glycosides, which have been used to treat various health issues, such as constipation, hemorrhoids, and skin conditions. Tragacantha mossulensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or powder, with recommended dosages and usage varying depending on the specific application and individual health needs.
